title: Merging two sorted lists is correct (sorted + permutation)
statement_informal: Merging two sorted lists yields a sorted list that is a permutation of their concatenation — the heart of merge sort. merge is fuel-bounded (total length decreases). merge_correct: Sorted l1 -> Sorted l2 -> length l1 + length l2 <= fuel -> Sorted (merge fuel l1 l2) /\ Permutation (merge fuel l1 l2) (l1 ++ l2).

cost_model.notes: Each step compares the two heads and emits the smaller; <= |l1|+|l2| comparisons. Sortedness carries a lower bound (le_hd) through the recursion; permutation uses perm_skip + Permutation_middle.

references: Merge of two sorted runs (TAOCP Vol. 3 §5.2.4) — the core of merge sort. Full correctness: sorted + permutation. Cost is work-units/merge-comparisons.
